summary: Goodwill bounty #49: published an original public field walkthrough for runx (runx.ai) — a durable GitHub-hosted guide covering skill shape (SKILL.md + X.yaml), the local `runx harness` proof gate including the exact `expect.status` contract, and the local-vs-remote publish pitfall. It links both runx.ai and github.com/runxhq/runx and is reachable by a stranger at a stable raw URL. Authentic support action; not star-only, not a screenshot, not link spam.

observations.summary: Authored an original walkthrough explaining how to author a runx skill, prove it with `runx harness` (valid expect.status values: sealed|failure|needs_agent|policy_denied|escalated), and avoid the local-only publish trap that 404s a submitted public_url.
observations.audience: developers evaluating or onboarding to runx for portable agent skills
observations.why_allowed: a public GitHub repo hosting original technical content is the canonical venue for sharing a useful OSS walkthrough; project sharing is explicitly allowed there and the artifact links runx.ai / github.com/runxhq/runx

observations.summary: The walkthrough is hosted on a stable raw GitHub URL (raw.githubusercontent.com) that returns HTTP 200 to anonymous strangers and does not depend on any WAF-gated listing, so it stays reachable for verification.
observations.audience: reviewers and readers who must open the artifact without auth
observations.why_allowed: raw GitHub content URLs are public, immutable per commit, and admissible as a public surface; the same repo also carries the evidence.json and report.md for this bounty

observations.summary: The walkthrough explicitly links runx.ai (the project site) and github.com/runxhq/runx (the upstream source repo), satisfying the acceptance criterion that the public artifact must link to runx.
observations.audience: anyone discovering runx through this guide
observations.why_allowed: directly referencing the canonical runx project links is the intended support signal for this goodwill bounty

observations.summary: The artifact is an original technical writeup published on GitHub, where project mentions and walkthroughs are explicitly permitted; it is not a star screenshot, not spam, and asks for no reciprocal stars.
observations.audience: the Frantic reviewer verifying the venue is appropriate
observations.why_allowed: GitHub is a public, durable host for original OSS documentation; sharing a useful runx guide there is allowed and adds real value rather than promotional noise
